name: Barcelona
localTips: Dinner starts late — most locals eat after 21:00. Restaurants open at 20:00 are catering to visitors.
Get a T-casual metro card (10 rides) — it works on metro, bus and the airport train from El Prat via R2 Nord.
Keep bags zipped and in front on Las Ramblas and the metro — pickpocketing is the city's one real hazard.
'Menú del día' at lunch (13:30–15:30) gets you three courses with wine for €12–16 at places that charge triple at dinner.
Many Gaudí sites sell out days ahead — book Sagrada Família and Park Güell before you arrive, not on the day.

activities.title: Gothic Quarter labyrinth
activities.description: Roman walls, hidden plazas and the cathedral's gargoyles in a maze of medieval lanes — get deliberately lost between Plaça Reial and Santa Maria del Mar.

activities.title: Barceloneta beach & seafront
activities.description: Four kilometres of city beach with a palm-lined promenade running to the Port Olímpic's shimmering fish sculpture — swim, stroll or just watch.

activities.title: Bunkers del Carmel sunset
activities.description: Civil-war anti-aircraft bunkers on a hilltop with the definitive 360° view of Barcelona — the young local crowd brings snacks and guitars.

activities.title: Parc de la Ciutadella
activities.description: Rowboats on the lake, the extravagant Cascada fountain (a young Gaudí helped), and half the city picnicking on Sundays.

activities.title: La Boqueria market
activities.description: The iron-and-glass temple of Catalan food off Las Ramblas — jamón, juices and counter bars where chefs eat breakfast.

activities.title: Sagrada Família façades & Eixample walk
activities.description: Circle Gaudí's basilica to read the Nativity and Passion façades from the outside, then walk the chamfered grid of the Eixample past Modernisme gems.

activities.title: Montjuïc hill gardens & viewpoints
activities.description: Ride the funicular up (metro ticket works) and wander between gardens, the castle ramparts and harbour viewpoints all the way down.

activities.title: Magic Fountain light show
activities.description: The 1929 Font Màgica dances to music in jets of colored water beneath the Palau Nacional — kitsch, free and completely charming.

activities.title: Barcelona Cathedral & cloister geese
activities.description: The Gothic cathedral's shaded cloister keeps thirteen white geese, one for each year of Santa Eulàlia's life — free entry in the morning window.

activities.title: Gràcia village squares
activities.description: A former independent town of small squares, vermouth bars and zero tour groups — Plaça del Sol at dusk is the neighborhood's living room.

activities.title: Santa Maria del Mar basilica
activities.description: The purest Catalan Gothic church, built by the people of the Ribera in just 55 years — soaring, austere and moving in a way the cathedral isn't.

activities.title: El Born boutiques & Passeig del Born
activities.description: Barcelona's most stylish medieval quarter — independent designers, the old market hall's excavated ruins, and cocktail bars filling the old jousting street.

activities.title: MNAC steps & free Saturday afternoons
activities.description: The Palau Nacional's steps give the classic view down to Plaça d'Espanya; inside, a millennium of Catalan art is free Saturdays after 15:00.

activities.title: El Raval: MACBA plaza & vintage shops
activities.description: Skaters own the MACBA plaza, vintage shops line Carrer de la Riera Baixa, and the old hospital's Gothic courtyard hides a quiet garden.

activities.title: La hora del vermut
activities.description: Sunday noon vermouth is a Barcelona institution — house-made vermut on tap with olives and anchovy gildas at old bodegas like Quimet & Quimet.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'un vermut de la casa' and whatever conserva (tinned seafood) the bartender points at — that's the whole ritual. €3–5.

activities.title: Sant Pau Art Nouveau site
activities.description: Domènech i Montaner's fairytale hospital complex — mosaic domes and tiled pavilions that outshine most Gaudí sites, at a tenth of the crowds.
activities.insiderNote: It's a ten-minute walk up Avinguda Gaudí from the Sagrada Família — the avenue frames both monuments and almost no one makes the connection.

activities.title: Absinthe at Bar Marsella
activities.description: Open since 1820 and barely dusted since — Hemingway and Picasso drank absinthe under these same chandeliers in El Raval.
activities.insiderNote: Order 'una absenta', balance the sugar cube on the fork, drip water over it, and don't rush. Cash only, opens late.

activities.title: Sant Antoni market & Sunday book fair
activities.description: The beautifully restored iron market hall feeds the neighborhood all week; on Sundays its perimeter becomes a second-hand book and collectors' fair.
activities.insiderNote: The bars inside the market serve some of the best-value seafood tapas in the city — locals do breakfast here, not brunch.

activities.title: Concert at the Palau de la Música
activities.description: Hear anything — flamenco, quartets, guitar — under the stained-glass skylight of the world's most flamboyant concert hall.
activities.insiderNote: Cheap seats (from ~€20) still get you the full ceiling; the guided day tour costs nearly as much as a short evening concert.

activities.title: Carretera de les Aigües trail
activities.description: A flat gravel balcony path across the Collserola hills where locals run and cycle above the whole city grid — take the funicular from Peu del Funicular.
activities.insiderNote: Go within two hours of sunset: the city lights come on below you and the path stays busy enough to feel safe.

activities.title: Xurros con xocolata at a granja
activities.description: The old dairy bars ('granges') of Carrer Petritxol serve churros with chocolate thick enough to stand a spoon in — a winter ritual since the 1800s.
activities.insiderNote: Granja Dulcinea and La Pallaresa are the classics; locals dip melindros (sponge fingers) rather than churros — try both.

activities.title: Sagrada Família entry with tower access
activities.description: Gaudí's forest of columns and kaleidoscope light — the interior silences even the skeptics. Add a tower lift for the spiral descent.
activities.bookingQuery: Sagrada Familia fast track ticket with tower access Barcelona

activities.title: Park Güell timed entry
activities.description: The mosaic salamander, the serpentine bench and the gingerbread gatehouses — Gaudí's hillside garden city with the city spread below.

activities.title: Casa Batlló immersive visit
activities.description: Gaudí's dragon-backed house of bone balconies and underwater light — the augmented-reality tablet actually earns its keep here.

activities.title: El Born evening tapas & wine tour
activities.description: Hop between counter bars with a local guide — jamón carving, cava, and the difference between a pintxo and a tapa, settled once and for all.
activities.bookingQuery: evening tapas and wine walking tour Barcelona

activities.title: Flamenco night at a tablao
activities.description: Raw, close-quarters flamenco at venues like Tablao Cordobés on Las Ramblas — an hour of stamping intensity you feel through the floor.

activities.title: Sunset sailing from Port Vell
activities.description: Two hours under sail along the city coastline with vermouth in hand — the skyline from the water is Barcelona's most underrated view.

activities.title: Camp Nou / Barça immersive tour
activities.description: Trophy room, tunnel and pitch-side at one of football's cathedrals — the museum is a pilgrimage even for neutral fans.
activities.bookingQuery: FC Barcelona museum and stadium tour ticket

activities.title: Montserrat monastery day trip
activities.description: A serrated mountain, a cliff-hugging monastery, the Black Madonna and boys' choir — an hour from the city by rack railway or cable car.
activities.bookingQuery: Montserrat monastery day trip from Barcelona

activities.title: Paella cooking class with Boqueria shopping
activities.description: Shop the market with a chef, then build a proper socarrat crust and drink sangría you made yourself — lunch solved, skill acquired.
activities.bookingQuery: paella cooking class with market visit Barcelona

activities.title: Festa Major de Gràcia street festival
activities.description: Each August Gràcia's residents transform their streets into competing handmade universes — dragons, oceans, space stations — with concerts every night.
